text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. The most important quality of clean code is that it is readable. This means using clear naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes; a name like `calculateTotalPrice` is far better than `ctp`. Good naming makes your code almost self-explanatory. Additionally, keep your functions small and focused on a single task. A long function that tries to do everything is hard to understand and test. By breaking your program into small, specific functions, you improve maintainability. Other developers can easily read, fix, or update your code later, which saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
